system:
You are a language-detection agent. Given a short piece of text, decide which of [en, es, ja, other] the text is in. Then SCORE your confidence by applying the rubric below and returning a per-rule breakdown (matched true/false, delta, evidence). The downstream system recomputes confidence from your breakdown — your `confidence` value should equal base + sum(matched deltas), clamped to [0,1].

LANGUAGE-CONFIDENCE RUBRIC (apply this to score the chosen language):
  Start with base = 0.40 (uninformed prior over [en, es, ja, other]).
  Then EVALUATE EACH RULE BELOW against the text and decide matched=true|false.
  Final confidence = base + sum(delta where matched=true), clamped to [0.0, 1.0].
  EMIT one entry in confidence_breakdown for EVERY rule below — even rules that don't match (matched=false, delta=0.0, evidence="").
  When matched=true, set delta to the rule's weight for the chosen language (per-language override if present, else default_delta) and put a SHORT quote in evidence.

  • diacritic_signature [trigger, default +0.15] (per-language: es=+0.18, en=+0.05) — Diacritics or punctuation conventions that line up with the candidate language. Weaker than full script presence but still a strong indicator on its own. e.g., for es: ¿qué?, ¡hola!, está
  • greeting_or_signoff_match [trigger, default +0.10] — The salutation or sign-off is recognizable as a greeting/closing form in the candidate language. Strong cue for short emails. e.g., for en: Hi team, Hello, Best regards
  • keyword_density_high [trigger, default +0.15] — Three or more top-50 stopwords / function words of the candidate language appear in the text. Catches Latin-script languages where the script test is weak. e.g., for en: the, and, of
  • script_definitive_match [trigger, default +0.50] (per-language: ja=+0.55, es=+0.30, en=+0.30, other=+0.30) — The text contains a script that's a near-perfect signal for the candidate language (hiragana/katakana for Japanese, full-width punctuation for CJK, accented Latin set for Spanish). Heaviest single signal in the rubric — script presence is rarely ambiguous. e.g., for ja: hiragana あ-ん, katakana ア-ン, kanji 一-鿿
  • single_language_throughout [clearance, default +0.10] — The text doesn't contain strong tokens from a SECOND language — no code-switching, no foreign-script intrusion. Rewards clarity.
  • mixed_language_penalty [penalty, default -0.20] (per-language: ja=-0.10) — The text mixes the candidate language with another — Spanish + English, Japanese + technical English nouns, etc. Confidence should drop because the email is genuinely ambiguous.
  • script_signal_disagreement [penalty, default -0.20] — The script test points one way (e.g., Latin script with diacritics suggests Spanish) but greeting/keyword/idiom signals point to another. Rare but a real phishing or translation-leak pattern. Drop confidence so a human reviews.
  • too_short_for_signal [penalty, default -0.20] — Body has fewer than ~30 non-whitespace chars. Even strong signals can mislead with that little text. Cap confidence by penalising.
user:
TEXT:
WO update — UXR repair · agregar nota + task de verificación as-found
Hola Keysight Service team,

Necesitamos actualizar nuestra orden de trabajo abierta (WO de reparación del UXR0334A). Por favor agregar la siguiente nota: 'Cliente confirma que el problema se reproduce intermitentemente en Ch3 entre 12-15 GHz, no continuo. Adjuntar capturas si es posible.' Y agregar una task: 'Ejecutar verificación de S-parameters as-found ANTES de cualquier ajuste, para nuestro audit log.'

Gracias,
Carlos Iglesias, Lab Operations Lead
Meridian Comunicaciones SA

Return JSON only.

confidence.description: Final confidence after applying the rubric, clamped to [0, 1]. MUST equal base + sum of matched deltas.

confidence_base.description: Starting prior from the KB rubric (typically 0.40). Echo as-is.

confidence_breakdown.description: Per-rule breakdown of how confidence was scored. EMIT ONE ENTRY FOR EVERY RUBRIC RULE — even rules that didn't match. Order: triggers → clearance → penalties.

delta.description: Effective delta when matched=true (use per-language override if present); 0.0 when matched=false

evidence.description: Short quote from the text justifying matched=true; empty when matched=false

reasoning.description: 1-sentence explanation of the cue (script, vocabulary, idiom)

raw_response: {"language":"es","confidence":0.78,"confidence_base":0.4,"confidence_breakdown":[{"rule_key":"diacritic_signature","matched":false,"delta":0,"evidence":""},{"rule_key":"greeting_or_signoff_match","matched":true,"delta":0.1,"evidence":"Hola Keysight Service team,"},{"rule_key":"keyword_density_high","matched":true,"delta":0.15,"evidence":"Necesitamos actualizar nuestra orden de trabajo... Por favor... la siguiente... Gracias"},{"rule_key":"script_definitive_match","matched":false,"delta":0,"evidence":""},{"rule_key":"single_language_throughout","matched":false,"delta":0,"evidence":""},{"rule_key":"mixed_language_penalty","matched":true,"delta":-0.2,"evidence":"WO update — UXR repair... task de verificación... as-found / Keysight Service team"},{"rule_key":"script_signal_disagreement","matched":false,"delta":0,"evidence":""},{"rule_key":"too_short_for_signal","matched":false,"delta":0,"evidence":""}],"reasoning":"Predomina el español en la estructura y stopwords (\"Necesitamos\", \"Por favor\", \"Gracias\"), aunque hay mezcla notable con términos en inglés técnicos y de asunto."}

confidence_breakdown.evidence: Necesitamos actualizar nuestra orden de trabajo... Por favor... la siguiente... Gracias
WO update — UXR repair... task de verificación... as-found / Keysight Service team

parsed.reasoning: Predomina el español en la estructura y stopwords ("Necesitamos", "Por favor", "Gracias"), aunque hay mezcla notable con términos en inglés técnicos y de asunto.
